wedge: SuperCharts closes the loop TradingView deliberately leaves open: chart → script → backtest → alert → Telegram → live MT5 execution in one owned stack — collapsing the ~$1,000–1,500/yr TradingView + PineConnector + VPS pile that every algo crypto/forex trader currently duct-tapes together into a single $600/yr terminal.
positioning: For algo-leaning crypto and forex traders who execute through MT5, SuperCharts is the charting terminal that actually trades: unlike TradingView — which stops at a webhook and makes you rent a $39/mo bridge plus your own VPS — SuperCharts ships native MT5 automation with a built-in risk stack (position sizing, portfolio heat, max-drawdown breaker), Telegram-first alerts with chart snapshots, ungated order-flow/SMC tooling, and a scriptable full-history screener, at one flat price with no per-feature tier ladder.

gapMatrix.tradingview: Gold standard; Premium ($59.95/mo) gives 8 charts/tab, bar replay, 20K historical bars; volume footprint now on all paid tiers; everything autosaves silently (tradingview.com/pricing).
gapMatrix.supercharts: Genuine near-parity, verified by the 2026-07-10 audit: custom Canvas engine, 22 chart types, multi-pane grids, replay, log/percent scales, server-persisted drawings, footprint/DOM/tape/heatmap included, 0 console errors, 406 tests. BUT workspace is lost on refresh — manual named layouts only (rebuild slice S3 planned in docs/terminal-rebuild-plan.md).

gapMatrix.tradingview: ~150 built-ins + 100K+ community scripts; hard per-chart caps by tier (2/5/10/25/50 indicators on Free→Ultimate).
gapMatrix.supercharts: 38 classic + 11 SMC + full order-flow suite (footprint w/ imbalance & absorption, DOM ladder, tape, liquidity heatmap, volume/market profile, OI) with NO per-chart caps and no tier gates; known holes vs TV's classic set (DEMA/TEMA/VWMA — punchlist INC-10). Order flow honestly Binance-only.

gapMatrix.tradingview: Pine v6: huge official docs, 100K published scripts, every AI assistant knows it; but sandboxed with its own caps (Pine Screener ignores it beyond 500 bars, 40 security-call limit for chart screeners).
gapMatrix.supercharts: PulseScript is real and audited (~120 tests; CodeMirror editor with highlighting, backtester, optimizer, walk-forward, saved scripts, onTf no-repaint MTF; honest gaps in docs/pulsescript-parity.md: no match/UDT/maps, script alert() captured but delivery not wired). Docs exist ONLY as repo markdown — zero user-facing documentation. An undocumented original language is a liability, not a moat.

gapMatrix.tradingview: Free stock/crypto/forex screeners plus Pine Screener (scan a watchlist with your own script) — but Pine Screener computes on only the last 500 bars, one indicator per scan, favorites-only, fixed timeframe list, one scan at a time (tradingview.com/support/solutions/43000742436).
gapMatrix.supercharts: The right-rail 'Scanner' tab is only a Binance 24h top-movers list — not a screener at all (PHASE0-BASELINE.md). This is the user's explicit ask AND the best offensive move: a PulseScript-powered screener over an expanded crypto+FX catalog with full-history compute and multi-condition filters beats Pine Screener's caps precisely because the universe is small.

gapMatrix.tradingview: 400 active alerts on Premium; pricing page now lists alert durations of 1–2 months (expiry); delivery = app/email/webhook, no native Telegram; webhooks require Plus ($29.95/mo) or above.
gapMatrix.supercharts: Production-proven engine — 48 live 1d EMA(5)×EMA(10) alerts running today; dual-MA + indicator-condition alerts, no count caps, Telegram-native with chart-snapshot PNGs and chat auto-detect, web delivery, anti-flood watermark, plus an inbound webhook receiver that can ingest TradingView's own alerts.

gapMatrix.tradingview: No native MT5 execution — the accepted path is TV alert → webhook (Plus+) → PineConnector at $39/mo Starter / $79/mo for 3 accounts (pineconnector.com) + a self-run VPS ($15–30/mo); third-party reviews put a realistic bridge setup at $470–750/yr before the TV subscription.
gapMatrix.supercharts: Native MT5 TCP bridge (:7878) + intent router + signal runner + bulk automation across watchlists, plus a risk stack TV doesn't offer at any tier: position sizer, portfolio heat, P&L attribution, max-DD breaker that halts the runner, paper trading. One must-fix inside the wedge: MT5 WS events broadcast unscoped to all sockets (ws-gateway.ts:112-122, hardcoded 'demo' user) — slice S5 before any multi-user exposure.

gapMatrix.tradingview: ~100M users, ideas feed, 100K+ published scripts, social graph, script marketplace — unassailable for a solo founder.
gapMatrix.supercharts: None. Strategy share links (/s/<token>) exist; the founder's algo-trading YouTube channel (MT5/forex audience) is the real distribution asset and the honest community substitute.

gapMatrix.tradingview: First-class native iOS/Android apps with full charting and alerts.
gapMatrix.supercharts: Desktop web only; mobile-responsive is roadmap Phase 5 #23. The honest mobile story for this ICP: Telegram delivery — alert + chart snapshot lands on the phone; the automation keeps running server-side.

gapMatrix.tradingview: Essential $14.95 / Plus $29.95 / Premium $59.95 per month after the April-2026 rises; annual ≈ $155/$359/$719 per year; Ultimate ~$200/mo (tradingview.com/pricing, supa.is, financialtechwiz.com). Algo users additionally pay PineConnector + VPS.
gapMatrix.supercharts: $400/6mo ($66.67/mo) and $600/12mo ($50/mo) — but Stripe is scaffolded, not live; auth is a single-user demo stub; no hosting. The product literally cannot take money or serve a second user today.

gapMatrix.tradingview: Thousands of markets — stocks, options, futures, indices, every major crypto exchange, licensed official feeds.
gapMatrix.supercharts: 48-symbol catalog: Binance WS (crypto, keyless), OANDA forex (user's own token via onboarding wizard), Yahoo fallback for FX/metals/indices, CSV import for anything else; order flow Binance-only and honestly labeled, never faked. Adequate providers for the ICP, but 48 symbols is too thin for a screener to mean anything — expand to the full Binance spot/perp list + all FX majors/crosses (both free with existing providers). Never chase stocks/options.

notDoing: Stock/options/futures data breadth — crypto + FX only; order flow stays Binance-only and honestly labeled rather than faked for other venues
Community platform (ideas feed, script marketplace, comments, social graph) — the YouTube channel plus existing /s/<token> strategy-share links ARE the community for v1
Native mobile apps or a mobile-responsive terminal — Telegram alerts with chart snapshots are the mobile surface; Phase 5 #23 stays deferred
Multi-user workspaces and embedded iframe charts (roadmap Phase 4 #18–19 stay deferred)
Matching Pine's 100K-script library — ship public docs + ~20 first-party PulseScript examples and the Pine→PulseScript migration table instead of a marketplace
Non-MT5 broker integrations (cTrader, IBKR, DXtrade, TV-style broker panel) — MT5 IS the wedge; don't dilute it
Seconds-based intervals / tick-chart parity chase (TV Essential+ has it; irrelevant to swing/algo ICP)
Fundamentals-based stock screener — screener v1 is technical/PulseScript-driven over the crypto+FX catalog only
Remaining cosmetic TV-parity tail (INC-16/17/18 pane resize, legend drag, magnet feel) beyond what rebuild slices S1–S8 already schedule — none of it blocks the wedge
pricingRead: TV Premium is $59.95/mo month-to-month and ~$599–719/yr billed annually after the April-2026 price rises (sources: tradingview.com/pricing — INR page shows Premium ₹4,195/mo ≈ $50 annual-equivalent and Ultimate ₹17,333 ≈ $200/mo; supa.is/article/tradingview-essential-vs-plus-vs-premium-which-plan-2026; financialtechwiz.com/post/how-much-is-tradingview). Read on the intended tiers: $400/6mo = $66.67/mo — priced ABOVE TV Premium's monthly sticker while lacking TV's data breadth, mobile apps, community, and brand trust; that anchor loses every head-to-head and should not ship. $600/12mo = $50.00/mo — exactly TV Premium annual money, defensible ONLY when sold against the ICP's real stack: TV Plus/Premium ($360–719/yr; webhooks need Plus+) + PineConnector ($39/mo Starter ≈ $312–468/yr, pineconnector.com) + VPS ($15–30/mo ≈ $180–360/yr) = roughly $1,000–1,500/yr for chart→MT5 automation (signalforge-ai.com pegs a realistic bridge-only setup at $470–750/yr before the TV sub). Recommendations: (1) keep $600/12mo as the flagship and market it exclusively as 'replace the whole stack — one vendor, roughly half price, execution included'; (2) cut the 6-mo tier to ~$330–350 or kill it so the ladder pushes annual — never let a $66.67/mo number appear next to TV's $59.95; (3) launch as founding-member pricing with a 14–30 day trial (TV gives 30 days; PineConnector gives 14) because a solo product at TV-Premium money must buy trust with a trial and an uptime story for alerts + MT5; (4) caveat everything: Stripe isn't live, auth is a demo stub, and the product runs on the founder's laptop — until slices S3/S5 + billing + hosting land, this pricing is aspiration, not a plan.
